channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Teacher Dismissed After Sexual Relationship with Student Results in Pregnancy
item.description: A teacher at Lotus Gardens Secondary School has been dismissed after an arbitrator found him guilty of engaging in a sexual relationship with a 17-year-old student, resulting in her pregnancy. The Edu...

item.title: Trump to Deliver Speech at Mount Rushmore During US 250th Anniversary
item.description: President Donald Trump is set to deliver a significant speech at Mount Rushmore National Memorial as the United States commemorates its 250th anniversary. The event, scheduled for July 4, 2026, marks ...

item.title: Fourth of July air show in Washington features historic military aircraft display
item.description: The article discusses the launch of a mobile application designed to help spectators track the Fourth of July aerial flyover in Washington, D.C., which will feature a nine-hour display of military air...

item.title: Oil Prices Stabilize Amid U.S.-Iran Ceasefire Uncertainty
item.description: Global oil prices have remained within a narrow range of $71-$73 per barrel for ICE Brent, reflecting market fatigue with geopolitical tensions and a lack of clarity on the fragile U.S.-Iran ceasefire...

item.title: Iran and the US navigate post-war tensions as Ayatollah Khamenei's funeral plans unfold
item.description: The article details the aftermath of the Iran-US conflict, focusing on the public mourning for Ayatollah Khamenei, who was a central figure in Iran's political landscape. Teherán has announced plans f...

item.title: Severe thunderstorm warning disrupts Trump's planned Mount Rushmore speech in South Dakota
item.description: A severe weather system in South Dakota disrupted President Donald Trump’s scheduled appearance near Mount Rushmore, where he was expected to speak as part of a July 4 Independence Day celebration tie...

item.title: DOT Confirms Michelin Guide's Continued Presence in the Philippines Until 2028
item.description: The Department of Tourism (DOT) has confirmed that the MICHELIN Guide will remain in the Philippines until at least 2028, highlighting the country's growing reputation for world-class dining. The guid...

item.title: India’s role in shaping British strategy during the American War of Independence
item.description: The article explores the surprising and often overlooked role that events in colonial India played in shaping the outcome of the American War of Independence. It argues that military and economic deve...

item.title: Poland's Bargiel achieves milestone by skiing Pakistan's 8,000m peaks without oxygen
item.description: Polish ski mountaineer Andrzej Bargiel has made history by becoming the first person to complete ski descents from all five of Pakistan’s 8,000-meter peaks without supplemental oxygen. The 38-year-old...

item.title: NATO Summit Pledges $80 Billion in Military Aid for Ukraine
item.description: At the upcoming NATO summit in Ankara, European members and Canada will commit to providing Ukraine with $80 billion in military aid over two years, according to diplomats. This pledge, outlined in a ...

item.title: Cape Town Weekend Events: Music, Storytelling, and Sustainable Dining
item.description: The article highlights a series of events taking place in Cape Town over the weekend, focusing on music, storytelling, and shared dining experiences. Alistair Izobell celebrates his 50th birthday with...

item.title: Opposition Tensions Rise Over Gachagua's Alleged Support for Kalonzo in 2027 Election
item.description: The article details political tensions within Kenya's opposition ahead of the 2027 General Election, focusing on claims that Rigathi Gachagua has aligned with Kalonzo Musyoka. This alliance has sparke...

item.title: Ecuador's World Cup return: Fans celebrate historic victory after 28-year absence
item.description: Ecuador's national football team made a triumphant return to the World Cup after 28 years, sparking jubilant celebrations among fans both in their home country and abroad. The victory, achieved agains...

item.title: Nigerian News Headlines: Key Stories of the Day

item.title: MAGA activist promotes weather-control conspiracy theory involving liberals and Trump, drawing criticism
item.description: A recent political controversy emerged after a MAGA-affiliated activist suggested that extreme weather conditions in Washington, D.C. may have been artificially manipulated. Kylie Jane Kremer, known f...

item.title: Tanzania and Mozambique Strengthen Economic Cooperation for Regional Growth
item.description: Tanzania and Mozambique are collaborating to boost economic transformation in East Africa through strategic initiatives. The two nations are enhancing trade, investment, and cross-border cooperation v...

item.title: Sony's Shift to Digital Gaming Sparks Concern Among Developers and Publishers
item.description: Sony's decision to discontinue physical disc production for new PlayStation games starting in 2028 has sparked widespread dismay among game companies and developers. The move, which will transition al...

item.title: How a Victorian community is turning household food and garden waste into valuable compost
item.description: Across Australia, councils are grappling with how best to manage growing volumes of organic waste, which includes food scraps, garden clippings and timber. With around 14.6 million tonnes of organic m...

item.title: Federal aid and military support launched for Manitoba flood recovery
item.description: Canada's Minister of Emergency Management has approved federal assistance for flood-affected regions in western Manitoba, including military logistical support and volunteer deployment. The Canadian A...

item.title: Durban July 2026: Key contenders and historic moments set for thrilling race
item.description: The 130th edition of the Hollywoodbets Durban July, South Africa's most prestigious horse race with a R10m prize purse, is set to deliver excitement after significant changes to the field. The withdra...
